sentence: Here is the SOAP note based on the transcript:
Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jack, is a 30-year-old male who was involved in a car accident approximately one week ago, where he hit the back of his car and experienced neck pain.
He reports the pain is a 7 out of 10 in severity, not constant, but worsens with neck movement.
He has tried ibuprofen with minimal relief.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of playing football years ago, but no issues with his neck.
Review of systems: The patient denies headaches, hearing problems, or visual disturbances.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, pain with flexion, extension, rotation, and lateral bending.
He has no pain on palpation of the back or shoulders.
Test Results: The patient's X-ray results show no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has neck strain.
Problem list: Neck strain; Active.
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for anti-inflammatory purposes.
I also prescribed Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to be conservative with neck movement and to try to relax his neck.
I ordered an MRI to rule out a concussion or nerve impingement.
I referred the patient to physical rehab or a chiropractor to further evaluate and treat his neck strain.
Follow-up: I instructed the patient to follow up after receiving the results of the MRI.
